notes.en: Warm temperatures up to 40 °C, humidity above 90 %, excessive nitrogen, dense plant populations, cloudy weather and shade all favor infection.

description.en: Lesions develop near the waterline on leaf sheaths — 1–3 cm, white-centred with green, brown and orange banding. Sclerotia (4–5 mm) form on leaf surfaces, initially white then turning brown or purplish-brown at maturity.

description.quote: Lesions develop near the water level on leaf sheaths, measuring 1-3 cm with white in the center, banded with green, brown, and orange coloration. Sclerotia (4-5 mm structures) form on leaf surfaces, initially white but turning brown or purplish brown at maturity.

favored_conditions.quote: high temperature conditions reaching 40°C, moisture levels exceeding 90%, excessive nitrogen application, and dense plant populations. Cloudy weather and shade also promote infection.
